inputs.prompt: Title: The Waiting Room. Format: Feature documentary, 90 min. Subject: A documentary set entirely inside a visa processing centre, following applicants from five different countries over three months as they wait for decisions that will change their lives. Each person's story reveals a different face of immigration policy. Target: Festival circuit. Stage: In production.
Title: Backyard. Format: Podcast series. Subject: Urban gardening movement. Audience: general.

Title: The Interpreters. Format: Documentary series, 4 x 52 min. Subject: Court interpreters across the EU who translate the most emotionally charged testimony in asylum hearings, war crimes tribunals, and domestic violence cases. What they carry home. Target: European public broadcasters. Stage: Development.

Title: The Bone Setters. Format: Documentary series, 3 x 45 min. Subject: Traditional bone-setting practitioners in rural India who treat fractures without X-rays or medical degrees, passed down through family lineages. The series follows three practitioners across Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, and Assam as modern hospitals arrive in their villages. Target: International streaming platforms. Stage: Development.

Title: The World. Format: Documentary. Subject: Basically everything happening in the world right now — politics, technology, climate, culture, food, music, sports, religion, economics. I want it to be like a time capsule of 2026. Target: Everyone. Stage: Idea.

Title: The Night Runners. Format: Feature documentary, 80 min. Subject: Long-distance runners in the Rift Valley training before dawn by headlamp on red-dirt roads, following four athletes from rural training camps through qualification races to the continental championships. The film explores the economics of athletics — who profits when a runner succeeds, and what happens to the hundreds who don't. Target: Sports documentary festivals and African broadcasters. Stage: Production, 4 months of footage shot.

Title: The Ink Makers. Format: Short documentary, 35 min. Subject: The last three artisanal ink manufacturers in the Middle East who produce traditional calligraphy inks using centuries-old recipes. As commercial inks dominate, these craftspeople supply a shrinking community of master calligraphers. Target: Arts documentary strand. Stage: Pre-production.
